behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real speech-model diarization output (speaker labeling quality), which a stubbed transcript would hide. This is a single model-quality boundary, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real audio sample with known speaker turns through the actual speech-to-text API and assert the transcript includes stable, correct speaker labels that can be mapped to attendee identities for downstream summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic on transcript text and can fail entirely within one parser/function boundary. Rejected higher levels because the behavior text is only about extraction into structure, with no required real API/OS/model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures containing explicit assignments (including edge phrasing) and assert exact structured outputs for assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This checks whether the real LLM produces complete action-item coverage per assignee; deterministic mocks would mask omission failures. It is primarily one model-output-quality step, not inherently multi-agent workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Use real meeting transcripts with known assignee-task ground truth, call the production summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at local module handoff (decision extractor output into schema composer/routing), so wiring multiple modules is the minimum realistic boundary. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text says "identified decisions" and focuses on routing destination, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire extractor + summary-structure builder with fixture inputs containing pre-identified decision objects and verify decisions land only in the `decisions` schema field, not narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real model judgment for decision salience/extraction and section placement from transcript content. A stubbed model response would trivially pass and hide true misses.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing known key decisions through the live summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing across segments plus merge coherence, with repeated model-in-the-loop stages; single-step or mocked tests miss cross-segment degradation and handoff errors.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real long-audio pipeline (>60 min): segment, transcribe/summarize each segment in sequence, merge outputs, then validate global coherence and that cross-segment action items/decisions are preserved in final structured summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end pipeline requirement with multiple real model-backed stages and external service interactions; only workflow-level execution can expose orchestration/handoff failures across the full chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting recording through production-like upload → transcription → summarization → structured delivery path and assert successful completion, schema-valid output, and no manual recovery steps.
